steps.title: Use the y-intercept
steps.content: The point $(0,2)$ lies on the line, so the y-intercept is $b=2$. With slope $4$ and y-intercept $2$, the function is $f(x)=4x+2$.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $f(x)=2x+42$: gives $f(0)=42 eq 2$. Eliminate.
  • B) $f(x)=32x+36$: gives $f(0)=36 eq 2$. Eliminate.
  • C) $f(x)=4x+2$: gives $f(0)=2$ and $f(8)=34$. Keep.
  • D) $f(x)=8x+2$: gives $f(8)=66 eq 34$. Eliminate.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
